articles.title: Analysts Have Conflicting Sentiments on These Consumer Cyclical Companies: Lululemon Athletica (LULU), Tesla (TSLA) and Amazon (AMZN)
articles.summary: Analysts are issuing varied ratings for key consumer cyclical companies. Lululemon Athletica and Tesla both received "Hold" ratings with modest upside potential, while Amazon was given a "Buy" rating with a more significant expected upside. These ratings reflect differing expert opinions on the near-term performance of these prominent stocks.

articles.title: Musk Says Tesla Has Begun Production of Its Cybercab Robotaxi
articles.summary: Elon Musk announced on social media site X that Tesla has started manufacturing its Cybercab robotaxi. This launch comes as Tesla faces a slump in global sales and fulfills a long-standing promise for the electric-vehicle maker. The Cybercab is intended to be a part of Tesla's Robotaxi network, operating with a mix of autonomous vehicles and those with human safety monitors.

articles.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Consumer Cyclical Companies: Tractor Supply (TSCO) and Tesla (TSLA)
articles.summary: Mizuho Securities maintained a Buy rating on Tractor Supply (TSCO) with a $50.00 price target, while Jefferies reiterated a Hold rating on Tesla (TSLA) with a target of $350.00. The analyst consensus for Tractor Supply is a Moderate Buy with a 26.8% upside, and for Tesla, it is a Hold with a 6.1% upside. The article details recent analyst updates for both consumer cyclical companies.

articles.title: Elon Musk vs. Plug Power Stock: Here's What the CEO of Tesla Thinks About Hydrogen Energy
articles.summary: This article explores Elon Musk's views on hydrogen energy, particularly in relation to companies like Plug Power. Musk has repeatedly expressed skepticism about hydrogen fuel for transportation, favoring lithium-ion batteries. The piece also discusses the high cost of hydrogen fuel compared to traditional and other renewable energy sources, concluding that its widespread viability is years away and companies like Plug Power are reliant on government subsidies.

articles.title: Tesla's stock surrenders gains after earnings. Here's what to know.
articles.summary: Tesla's shares gave back their initial gains following its first-quarter earnings report. Although cars remain its primary revenue source, investor focus is shifting towards its robotaxi and humanoid robot ventures. A Jefferies analyst noted that while there were positive aspects like a high automotive backlog, nothing in the earnings report was "groundbreaking."

articles.title: Bay Area Tesla owner files lawsuit against company over ‘self-driving' car promises
articles.summary: A Bay Area Tesla owner has filed a lawsuit against the company, alleging that it has not delivered on its "self-driving" car promises. The owner, Tom Losavia, spent $100,000 on a Model S and an additional $8,000 for the self-driving software package but claims the technology is not fully self-driving, nine years after his purchase. He is seeking a refund for the software and an order to prevent Tesla from marketing its software as self-driving, pointing to similar lawsuits globally.

He is seeking a refund for the software and an order to prevent Tesla from marketing its software as self-driving, pointing to similar lawsuits globally.

articles.title: Tesla's First-Mover Edge Is Slipping—And Waymo Is Closing In
articles.summary: Tesla's initial advantage in the robotaxi market is diminishing as Waymo focuses on commercial deployment and scaling operations. While Tesla has a large fleet and manufacturing scale, its robotaxi rollout is still limited, leading to concerns that its technical lead might not translate into operating revenue as quickly as rivals. The coming 12-24 months will be crucial, with success determined by fleet size, geographic reach, operating economics, and safety.

articles.title: Ross Gerber Says He's A 'Tesla Lover, Elon Critic' - Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA)
articles.summary: Ross Gerber, CEO of Gerber Kawasaki, labels himself a "Tesla lover" but an "Elon critic," arguing he is a "Tesla Hero" who cares about the company's true mission. He believes Elon Musk's political views are damaging to the Tesla brand, a public company, and should be kept private. Despite his criticisms, Gerber maintains that Tesla is an amazing company, though his firm, AdvisorShares Gerber Kawasaki ETF (NYSE: GK), no longer holds Tesla shares.

articles.title: EXCLUSIVE: Ross Gerber Says He's A 'Tesla Lover, Elon Critic' - Why He Sees Himself As The Tesla Hero And Not A Villain
articles.summary: Ross Gerber, CEO of Gerber Kawasaki, describes himself as a "Tesla lover" and "Elon critic," arguing that he is a "Tesla Hero" for his past support and current concerns. He believes Elon Musk's political views are damaging to Tesla as a public company, preventing it from reaching its full potential. Gerber emphasizes that a CEO's personal viewpoints should not define a public company's brand.

articles.title: Move Over, Nvidia: Why Jim Cramer Says the AI Boom Is Finally Hitting Intel
articles.summary: Jim Cramer suggests that Intel and AMD are poised to benefit from the next phase of AI demand, as the bottleneck shifts from NVIDIA GPUs to CPUs. This comes as Elon Musk backs Intel's 14A manufacturing process for Tesla's in-house AI chips. Intel's stock shows strong technical indicators, trading near its 52-week highs, with an upcoming earnings report expected to be a major catalyst.

articles.title: Financial Markets Morning Commentary
articles.summary: This morning commentary discusses minor movements in economic 10-year futures, yields, and S&P futures, despite increased vessel seizures in the Strait of Hormuz by Iran and interceptions by the US. The article details economic news including initial jobless claims, Tesla's Q1 revenue, and airline profit warnings due to high jet fuel costs, alongside geopolitical updates on the Strait of Hormuz, the US interception of Iranian oil tankers, and Russian wealth increases. Energy markets show mixed results for WTI and Brent crude, and metals markets react to geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Tesla Reveals Mysterious $2 Billion AI Hardware Deal in SEC Filing
articles.summary: Tesla has disclosed a mysterious acquisition of an AI hardware company for up to $2 billion in common stock and equity awards, with much of it tied to performance milestones. This deal, revealed in an SEC filing, underscores Elon Musk's increasing pivot of his companies towards AI, with significant capital expenditure increases for AI initiatives at Tesla and ambitious AI market estimations from SpaceX. The acquisition's structure suggests an interest in unproven technology and talent retention, potentially related to chip development like Tesla's next-gen AI5 self-driving chip.

articles.title: Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) Given "Buy" Rating at TD Cowen
articles.summary: TD Cowen reiterated a "Buy" rating on Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) with a $490 price target, despite mixed sentiment from other analysts and recent Q1 results that missed revenue estimates. The company reported an adjusted EPS beat but warned of significant capital expenditure increases and potential negative cash flow for the remainder of the year. Insider selling has also been noted, with directors and the CFO reducing their positions in the last 90 days.

Insider selling has also been noted, with directors and the CFO reducing their positions in the last 90 days.

articles.title: Apple supplier STMicroelectronics’ shares surge after strong quarter
articles.summary: Shares of Apple supplier STMicroelectronics surged after the European chipmaker reported solid first-quarter sales and projected increased revenue growth from artificial intelligence. The company, which also supplies Tesla and SpaceX, saw revenue jump 23% year-over-year to $3.10 billion, surpassing guidance and analyst estimates. Despite a lower net profit compared to the previous year, strong demand in personal electronics and a focus on AI are expected to drive future growth, moving past the slowdown in its automotive business.
